subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Demonstrate skills that relate to personal and food hygiene to self
indicator_description: Practice good grooming
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by good grooming and relate it to personal hygiene. [cite: 2811]
2. Discuss good grooming practices, in groups (e.g. proper sitting, walking, talking, eating manners). [cite: 2811]
3. Discuss the importance of good grooming (e.g. enhances personality and interpersonal relationships). [cite: 2811]
4. Demonstrate good grooming practices. [cite: 2811]
5. Write short messages on good grooming and tag them in and around the classroom. [cite: 2811]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Demonstrate skills that relate to personal and food hygiene to self
indicator_description: Observe appropriate food hygiene practices.
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by food hygiene. [cite: 2817]
2. Outline the appropriate food hygiene practices (e.g. wash hands, do not sneeze near food, separate clothes from cooking areas). [cite: 2817, 2819, 2820, 2821]
3. Watch a video on good food hygiene practices and discuss. [cite: 2822]
4. Clean workshop at close of work and dispose of refuse appropriately. [cite: 2823]
5. Demonstrate food hygiene practices in class and during practical lessons. [cite: 2824]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B9.1.2.1 Demonstrate skills that relate to personal, workshop and food laboratory safety
indicator_description: Describe procedures for reporting accidents and unsafe practices in school and in the laboratory/workshop
examplars: 1. Discuss the procedures (e.g. check for risk of danger, report to teacher). [cite: 2831, 2832]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B9.1.2.1 Demonstrate skills that relate to personal, workshop and food laboratory safety
indicator_description: Use appropriate personal protective equipment when working
examplars:
1. Identify the various personal protective equipment (e.g. goggles, ear muffs, gloves). [cite: 2835, 2836]
2. Discuss the importance of wearing personal protective equipment. [cite: 2837]
3. Demonstrate the use of personal protective equipment (eye, ear, hand, foot, and nose protection). [cite: 2838, 2839]
4. Project: Design and make personal protective equipment using compliant materials. [cite: 2840]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B9.1.2.1 Demonstrate skills that relate to personal, workshop and food laboratory safety
indicator_description: Maintain safe working environments
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by maintaining a safe working environment. [cite: 2844, 2845]
2. Discuss the importance of keeping the working environment safe. [cite: 2845, 2846]
3. Demonstrate safety practices (e.g. tag faulty equipment, repair frayed flexes). [cite: 2847]
4. Project work: Design posters to create awareness on safe working environments. [cite: 2848]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding and practice of environmental health in the school/home
indicator_description: Discuss the causes and prevention of poor sanitation in the school/home
examplars:
1. Discuss causes of poor sanitation (e.g. littering, poor disposal of waste). [cite: 2853, 2854]
2. Discuss ways of preventing poor sanitation (e.g. use of bins). [cite: 2855]
3. Undertake a project on keeping the school and community clean (e.g. clean-up exercise). [cite: 2856, 2857]
4. Undertake a project in recycling of waste. [cite: 2858]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.1.3.2 Demonstrate understanding of clean energy, and Improved Cookstoves (ICS) and their accompanying fuels
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by clean energy and improved cookstoves and fuels
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by clean energy. [cite: 2863, 2864]
2. Watch pictures and videos on improved cookstoves and traditional stoves for comparison. [cite: 2865]
3. Identify improved cookstoves and fuels and discuss the effects of clean energy use. [cite: 2866, 2867]
4. Search and present various improved cookstoves (e.g. Gyapa, gas stoves, pellets). [cite: 2868, 2869]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.1.3.2 Demonstrate understanding of clean energy, and Improved Cookstoves (ICS) and their accompanying fuels
indicator_description: Explain the benefits of improved cookstoves and fuels
examplars:
1. Discuss the benefits of improved cookstoves (e.g. save money, protect health). [cite: 2876, 2877]
2. Demonstrate the uses of improved cookstoves versus traditional stoves. [cite: 2878]
3. In groups, plan and organise a campaign to educate the community on improved cookstoves. [cite: 2879]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate skills in selecting compliant materials for making products and artefacts
indicator_description: Discuss the factors that influence the selection of compliant materials
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on properties of compliant materials and safe practices. [cite: 2885]
2. Discuss factors influencing selection (e.g. purpose, availability, designer skills). [cite: 2886, 2887]
3. Discuss processes involved (measuring, marking, cutting, folding, joining, finishing). [cite: 2888]
4. Make artefacts from compliant materials and appraise them in groups. [cite: 2888]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.2.2.1 Demonstrate skills in selecting resistant materials for making products and artefacts
indicator_description: Discuss the factors that influence the selection of resistant materials
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on properties and safe practices. [cite: 2894, 2895]
2. Discuss factors for selection (e.g. function, availability, skills). [cite: 2896, 2897]
3. Discuss processes involved (measuring, marking out, cutting/shaping). [cite: 2898]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.2.2.1 Demonstrate skills in selecting resistant materials for making products and artefacts
indicator_description: Discuss the reasons why resistant materials require particular techniques and tools for their safe handling and use
examplars:
1. Explain why specific tools are used for specific resistant materials (e.g. using wood saws for metal). [cite: 2901, 2902]
2. Relate correct safety precautions to appropriate processes (e.g. checking plane sharpness, hacksaw blade direction). [cite: 2903, 2904, 2905, 2906]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.2.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of using smart and modern materials for making products/artefacts
indicator_description: Discuss reasons for using smart and modern materials for making products/artefacts
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on smart and modern materials and their properties. [cite: 2910, 2911]
2. Compare uses of smart/modern versus compliant/resistant materials in a table. [cite: 2912]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.2.4.1 Demonstrate skills in selecting food commodities in meal preparation
indicator_description: Explain how to select food commodities used for meal preparation
examplars:
1. Revise the classification of food commodities. [cite: 2916, 2917]
2. Describe the qualities to look out for when buying animal, plant, or processed food products. [cite: 2918, 2919]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment
indicator_description: Identify and classify tools and equipment used for measuring and marking out
examplars:
1. Identify tools and equipment for measuring and marking out in building, wood, and metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2923]
2. Classify measuring and marking out tools under specific trade areas. [cite: 2924]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use the tools and equipment for measuring and marking out
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to use measuring and marking out tools in food lab, sewing, building, wood, and metal workshops. [cite: 2927]
2. Select appropriate tools for making specific products (e.g. wooden chair, sheet metal funnel, setting out walls, dresses, meals). [cite: 2928]
3. Prepare a chart showing activities and appropriate tools used and display for appraisal. [cite: 2928, 2929]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain measuring and marking out tools
examplars: 1. Demonstrate how to care for and maintain measuring and marking out tools (e.g. wash/clean after use, oil metal parts). [cite: 2934, 2935]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.2.1 Demonstrate the understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products
indicator_description: Identify and classify tools and equipment used for cutting and shaping
examplars:
1. Identify tools and equipment for cutting and shaping in building, wood, and metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2937]
2. Select appropriate tools for specific products (wooden tables, bottle opener, bricks/blocks, shirts, meals). [cite: 2938]
3. Write the procedure/steps involved in making the products. [cite: 2938]
4. Prepare and display a chart showing activities and appropriate tools used. [cite: 2939]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.2.1 Demonstrate the understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use shaping and cutting tools and equipment used for producing artefacts/products
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to use cutting and shaping tools for products in building, wood, metal, sewing, and food lab (e.g. biscuit cutting, designing fruits). [cite: 2947, 2948, 2949]
2. Write the procedure/steps involved and exhibit products for appraisal. [cite: 2949, 2950]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.2.1 Demonstrate the understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment used for making artefacts/ products
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain cutting and shaping tools for making artefacts/products.
examplars: 1. Demonstrate how to care for tools in specific trade areas (e.g. sterilising kitchen tools, oiling chisels and saws). [cite: 2954, 2955, 2956]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of materials, tools and equipment used for joining and assembling artefacts/products
indicator_description: Identify and classify joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/products
examplars:
1. Identify tools and equipment for joining in building, wood, and metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2959]
2. Classify joining tools for food lab, sewing, building, wood, and metal/plastic workshops. [cite: 2960]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of materials, tools and equipment used for joining and assembling artefacts/products
indicator_description: Demonstrate appropriate skills in the use of joining and assembling tools and equipment for making artefacts/products
examplars: 1. Demonstrate how to use materials and tools for joining specific products (e.g. bookshelf, metal scoop, wall, garment, meals). [cite: 2964, 2965, 2966]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of materials, tools and equipment used for joining and assembling artefacts/products
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain tools and equipment used for joining and assembling artefacts/products
examplars: 1. Demonstrate how to care for joining tools (e.g. wash after use, dry before storage). [cite: 2968, 2969, 2970]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.4.1.1 Demonstrate skills of selecting and purchasing kitchen essentials
indicator_description: Select and purchase suitable kitchen essentials to meet specific needs
examplars: 1. Discuss factors to consider in selection and purchasing (e.g. money, space, purpose, spare parts). [cite: 2972, 2973]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of finishes
indicator_description: Demonstrate the techniques of applying finishes to resistant materials
examplars:
1. Identify finishes and tools for finishing resistant materials. [cite: 2975, 2976]
2. Identify and use materials for preparing wood, metal, and wall surfaces. [cite: 2977, 2978]
3. Demonstrate the procedure for applying finishes to resistant materials. [cite: 2979]
4. Demonstrate how to wash finishing tools after use. [cite: 2980]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.3.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of application of finishes
indicator_description: Demonstrate basic skills and processes for finishing edges of articles in sewing
examplars:
1. Demonstrate edge finishing techniques (e.g. lacing, facing, scalloping). [cite: 2985, 2986]
2. Display specimens for appraisal. [cite: 2986]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of mechanisms in projects construction
indicator_description: Identify mechanisms used in for making products/ artefacts
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by mechanisms. [cite: 2989, 2990]
2. Explore different mechanisms (e.g. pulley, gear, screw, crank) using ICT tools. [cite: 2991, 2992]
3. Identify environmental artefacts operating on mechanisms. [cite: 2993]
4. Research how mechanisms operate and present class findings. [cite: 2994, 2995]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of mechanisms in projects construction
indicator_description: Describe the features and principles of operations of mechanisms
examplars:
1. Describe features using charts, models, or real objects. [cite: 2999]
2. Use diagrams to illustrate mechanism operations (e.g. rack and pinion, cams). [cite: 3000, 3001]
3. Discuss advantages and disadvantages of various mechanisms. [cite: 3002]
4. Watch videos on mechanisms in operation and discuss. [cite: 3003]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of mechanisms in projects construction
indicator_description: Design and make simple school technology projects using two or more of the mechanisms
examplars:
1. Identify projects (e.g. wall clocks, toy cars, wind turbines). [cite: 3009, 3010]
2. Identify materials and discuss choice of mechanisms. [cite: 3011, 3012]
3. Plan, design, make, and test product for functionability. [cite: 3013, 3014, 3015]
4. Write down and discuss observations in class. [cite: 3015]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of developing surfaces of objects for production/ manufacturing
indicator_description: Identify prisms and pyramids and discuss the importance of developing surfaces
examplars:
1. Name and draw common types of prisms and pyramids. [cite: 3023, 3024, 3025]
2. Differentiate between prisms and pyramids. [cite: 3026]
3. Discuss the importance of surface development (minimise waste, save time). [cite: 3027]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of developing surfaces of objects for production/ manufacturing
indicator_description: Develop surfaces of prisms using instruments
examplars:
1. Illustrate techniques of developing prisms. [cite: 3032]
2. Develop surfaces of simple objects to dimensions. [cite: 3033]
3. Cut, fold, and join developed surfaces to obtain objects (e.g. tins, match box). [cite: 3034, 3035, 3036]
4. Plan and mount an exhibition for appraisal. [cite: 3037]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of developing surfaces of objects for production/ manufacturing
indicator_description: Develop surfaces of pyramids using instruments
examplars:
1. Illustrate techniques and develop surfaces of types of pyramids. [cite: 3042, 3043, 3044]
2. Cut, fold, and join developed surfaces to obtain objects (e.g. funnel, Christmas hat). [cite: 3045, 3046]
3. Plan and mount an exhibition for appraisal. [cite: 3047]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of developing surfaces of objects for production/ manufacturing
indicator_description: Sketch and draw straight lines, curves, basic human figures, and make lettering
examplars:
1. Identify types of lines and discuss their applications. [cite: 3053, 3054, 3055]
2. Differentiate between lower and upper case letters and discuss lettering principles. [cite: 3056, 3057]
3. Prepare a title block. [cite: 3058]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing
indicator_description: Identify user requirements
examplars:
1. Explore community situations and discuss identified issues. [cite: 3063, 3064, 3065]
2. Identify community needs, wants, and lacks. [cite: 3065]
3. Analyse problems and their impact on community life. [cite: 3067]
4. Write a report and design brief. [cite: 3068]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing
indicator_description: Clarify user requirements
examplars:
1. Analyse the problem graphically and conduct research using questionnaires/interviews. [cite: 3076, 3077, 3078, 3079]
2. Analyse research and develop design specifications. [cite: 3080, 3081]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing
indicator_description: Generate Ideas
examplars:
1. Generate three possible ideas using freehand and write short descriptive notes. [cite: 3090, 3091]
2. Compare ideas with specifications. [cite: 3092]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of Designing
indicator_description: Develop the selected solution
examplars:
1. Identify the best design and indicate reasons for selection. [cite: 3097, 3098]
2. Examine and redesign the selected solution for the final design. [cite: 3099, 3100]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/ products/ meals
indicator_description: Plan and prepare food using dry methods of cooking
examplars:
1. Identify and describe basic dry methods of cooking (baking, grilling). [cite: 3105, 3106]
2. Discuss advantages, disadvantages, and principles of dry methods. [cite: 3108, 3109]
3. Plan and prepare a dish using dry methods. [cite: 3131]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/ products/ meals
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to clarify user requirements
examplars:
1. Study working drawings, cutting lists, and workshop environment. [cite: 3115, 3116, 3117]
2. Experiment with materials and tools to gain confidence. [cite: 3120]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/ products/ meals
indicator_description: Describe ways of using the natural building materials for production
examplars: 1. Discuss brick production from clay/laterite. [cite: 3127, 3128]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B9.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of gathering materials, tools and equipment for making/preparing meals and articles
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills in preparing food using the dry methods of cooking
examplars: 1. Prepare and display a dish each using baking and grilling. [cite: 3130, 3131, 3132]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B9.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of gathering materials, tools and equipment for making/preparing meals and articles
indicator_description: Create advanced articles using crocheting and embroidery stitches
examplars:
1. Review stitches and discuss crocheted/embroidered articles and their uses. [cite: 3135, 3136, 3137]
2. Make advanced articles (e.g. handbags, cardigans) and plan an exhibition. [cite: 3138, 3139]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B9.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of gathering materials, tools and equipment for making/preparing meals and articles
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to gather materials, tools, and equipment for making artefacts
examplars:
1. Examine design folio and check dimensions for accuracy. [cite: 3144, 3145]
2. Apply making operations in sequence and apply appropriate finish. [cite: 3146, 3147]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B9.5.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of gathering materials, tools and equipment for making/preparing meals and articles
indicator_description: Test and evaluate the made artefact
examplars:
1. Test artefact against specifications and prepare a check list for appraisal. [cite: 3152, 3153, 3154]
2. Rate quality and state suggested modifications. [cite: 3154, 3155]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.6.1.1 Demonstrate understanding about the changing nature of the workplace, the value of work to society, and the connection of work to the achievement of personal goals
indicator_description: Describe how the changing nature of the workplace can bring about global competition and technology
examplars:
1. Find out how workplace changes bring about global competition (e.g. automation, ICT, robots). [cite: 3160, 3161, 3162]
2. Discuss findings and write a summary. [cite: 3163]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.6.1.1 Demonstrate understanding about the changing nature of the workplace, the value of work to society, and the connection of work to the achievement of personal goals
indicator_description: Analyse the value of work to the individual and society in general
examplars: 1. Analyse value of work and discuss self-employment versus working for others. [cite: 3166, 3167, 3168, 3169, 3170]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B9.6.1.1 Demonstrate understanding about the changing nature of the workplace, the value of work to society, and the connection of work to the achievement of personal goals
indicator_description: Develop a career plan that would assist in the transition from school to eventual entry into a career option
examplars:
1. Discuss need for a career plan and identify steps (options, prioritise, compare). [cite: 3172, 3173, 3174]
2. Write and read summary to class. [cite: 3174]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B9.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of establishing and managing a small business enterprise
indicator_description: Describe how to start and run a small business
examplars:
1. Discuss starting a business (idea, copying, buying, modifying). [cite: 3179, 3180]
2. Write on legal forms of businesses in Ghana and discuss naming and licensing. [cite: 3181, 3182, 3183]
3. Summarise findings from registration agency visits. [cite: 3183]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B9.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of establishing and managing a small business enterprise
indicator_description: Explain how to manage resources of small business enterprises
examplars:
1. Think-pair-share on in-demand products/services. [cite: 3189]
2. Discuss resources (land, labour, capital, market, location) and management processes. [cite: 3190, 3191, 3192]
